metadata.seed: My mom died almost 2 years ago. Due to anxiety, i have a long term boyfriend but no other friends so i was pretty close with my mom. i miss her desperately

patient.query: My mom died almost 2 years ago. Due to anxiety, i have a long term boyfriend but no other friends so i was pretty close with my mom. i miss her desperately

neutral: Sudden bereavement due to life event (finding)

It's like my mind is constantly racing, but at the same time, I feel so stuck. I think about all the things I "should" be doing, but I can't bring myself to care or get motivated.

patient.query: Yeah, it really does feel heavy. I find myself avoiding plans or even just texting friends back. It’s like I want to reach out, but the thought of actually engaging feels like too much. I end up isolating myself more, which just makes everything feel worse. I’m stuck in this loop where the more I avoid, the more anxious I feel about interacting again.

the more I avoid, the more anxious I feel

patient.query: It’s really lonely. I mean, when I isolate myself, I start overthinking everything. I replay conversations in my head and think about how people must see me, like they’re judging me for not being "okay" or not being around. It just makes me feel more worthless, like I’m not even worth the effort to connect with.

triggers: thinking about how people see me
automatic_thoughts: I'm not worth the effort to connect

patient.query: Yeah, it really is tough. I feel like I’m constantly battling with those thoughts, almost like a tape that keeps replaying. Even if I want to reach out, that inner voice tells me I’m not worth it or that people will be disappointed in me. It just becomes easier to stay away and avoid the whole thing, even though I know it’s not helping.

automatic_thoughts: people will be disappointed in me
